# Questions in Livestream 198

Live Stream #198: Travel Tips For Visiting Purgatory

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=590" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:50</a> | How can you tell the difference between fair redaction criticism and "redacta-kadabra"? | He said literary and historical explanations should be exhausted first, with redaction used as a last resort apart from obvious spelling or grammatical updating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=930" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:30</a> | Why is there a revised German edition of Kitchen's *On the Reliability of the Old Testament* but not an English one? | He said the story lies in translation and publication history rather than in a straightforward new English revision by Kitchen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=1117" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">18:37</a> | Had Kenneth Kitchen been on any important digs? | No. He described Kitchen as more of a historian or "library archaeologist" who published and interpreted inscriptions rather than a field archaeologist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=1305" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:45</a> | What are your thoughts on William Dever's *Has Archaeology Buried the Bible?* | He said he did not think it was Dever's best work and thought Dever's older biases showed through. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=1374" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:54</a> | How would you evangelize a very educated Orthodox Jew? | He described a careful, respectful approach centered on Scripture rather than blunt point-scoring. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=1730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:50</a> | Does Proverbs 29:19-21 allow or encourage beating a slave? | He said no. Proverbs are wisdom aphorisms rather than case law, and verse 19 does not have to imply violence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=2236" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:16</a> | What are your opinions on the discovery of Thutmose II's tomb? | He deferred the question because it was the subject of the next Monday video. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=2513" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:53</a> | What avenue of biblical archaeology most needs work from someone entering the field? | He said Old Testament and cognate-field work still has enormous room for research, while New Testament archaeology offers less open ground. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=2722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:22</a> | Who is more credible, Kip Davis or Titus Kennedy? | He gave a joke answer implying neither option was credible in his view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=2748" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:48</a> | Is underwater archaeology a real field? | Yes. He answered that it is a genuine field and mentioned knowing a practitioner. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=3019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:19</a> | Do extinct animals have an afterlife or go to heaven? | He said it is possible and argued that people often have too small a conception of the heavens. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=3460" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:40</a> | Why would Paul set up churches in Pelusium if he was still a new believer? | He said Paul was not acting alone but under Barnabas's guidance, and Pelusium was nearby enough to be a plausible early mission point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=3611" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:11</a> | If early church services were not ideal, what would a perfect service include? | He said there is no perfect service form, but core elements include worship, Scripture reading, preaching, prayer, and sacraments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=3767" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:02:47</a> | What kind of teaching from Scripture would early church elders do? | He compared early services to didactic settings and said elders would teach from Scripture, especially reading the Old Testament in light of Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=4007" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:47</a> | Will there be sweet lobster chili in Egypt? | Probably not. He said lobster exists in some places, but Egyptians generally do not prepare it that way and do not use much spice in cooking. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=4203" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:03</a> | In 2 Kings 4:42, was it okay to eat the firstfruits bread? | Yes, because the prophet redirected it by divine instruction, and he treated it as typological of miraculous feeding. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=4621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:17:01</a> | What is Leviticus 16:12-13 about? | He said it describes ritual purification by incense smoke, a practice also known in other ancient Near Eastern and later traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=5050" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:10</a> | Have you had camel? | Yes. He said he had eaten camel in Luxor and recommended a specific restaurant experience there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=5283" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:03</a> | Why do people say Yom Kippur used lamb blood on the mercy-seat lid when Leviticus 16 says bull and goat? | He said they are confusing Yom Kippur with Passover; Yom Kippur covers sin temporarily, while Passover imagery points to final forgiveness in Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=5487" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:27</a> | Does Leviticus 17:3-4 forbid butchering your own meat, or is it about preventing false sacrifices? | He said the passage concerns sacrificial slaughter brought to the priests, not private slaughter for food. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=5913" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:33</a> | How did priests get vegetables? | He said vegetables could come through offerings and tithes, just as meat did. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=5960" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:20</a> | In Leviticus 17:16, what does it mean to bear guilt? | He said the term is better understood as iniquity or drift from the proper ritual path, illustrated by failing to purify after corpse contact. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=6399" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:39</a> | What are the restrictions of the Nazirite vow? | He said a Nazirite is distinct from a Nazarene and avoids fruit of the vine, corpse contact, and cutting hair, while the restriction is not a general alcohol ban. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=6485" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:48:05</a> | Would tomatoes count as fruit of the vine for a Nazirite? | He said the text was not considering tomatoes, but if taken technically as fruit of a vine they could fall under the category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=6670" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:51:10</a> | What is the principle of the Nazirite vow? | He said it is about maintaining ritual purity and dedicating a period of service to the Lord, not simply abstaining from intoxicants. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=6745" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:25</a> | Is mead acceptable for a Nazirite? | He treated it as a borderline or probably unacceptable case because honey is clean but not ritually pure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=6843" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:03</a> | Could Nazirites drink purple soda? | He said it depends on whether the flavoring is entirely artificial or derived from grapes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=7068" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:48</a> | Is John Walton correct that biblical honey was probably date syrup? | He said no; ancient people knew actual honey, and beekeeping is attested far back in the ancient Near East. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=7196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:56</a> | Are there academic libraries I can subscribe to for Bible commentaries after graduation? | He recommended checking local theological colleges or universities for community library cards, while noting database access is often restricted. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=7313" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:01:53</a> | Is ongoing university library access after graduation unusual? | Yes. He said most schools cut off alumni access quickly, especially to paid databases. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=7472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:04:32</a> | If *Joseph: King of Dreams* shows Joseph inventing irrigation, how early an exodus does that imply? | None. He said Egyptian irrigation and hydrology long predate Joseph by millennia, so this cannot date the exodus. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=7721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:41</a> | How were the pyramids made? | He answered that stone was moved largely by boat along Nile channels, then shifted overland by sledges. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=7881" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:11:21</a> | Were obelisks cut as single pieces? | Yes. He said Egyptian obelisks were single massive stones, with the unfinished Aswan obelisk showing the scale. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=8000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:20</a> | Is it weird or common to find Scripture harder to understand than other ancient Near Eastern literature? | He said it is common, partly because translations hide the complexity and debates in other ancient Near Eastern texts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=8245" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:25</a> | Should it bother me that the Gospels do not always mention where Jesus went? | No. He said the Gospel writers select details according to perspective and purpose, and recommended a Gospel synopsis for serious comparison. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=8577" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:57</a> | If the Ark of the Covenant were found today, would it still kill non-Levitical people? | Probably not. He said the lethal judgment was tied to God's presence with the ark before the Solomonic temple, not a permanent property of the object. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=9353" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:53</a> | How apparent are the two powers in heaven in the original text? | He said the idea is definitely present in the Old Testament, including Genesis, Isaiah, and the Psalms, despite some people ridiculing it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=9436" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:16</a> | How do you answer the critique that an omniscient God could have revealed germ theory, electricity, and vaccines instead of food and Sabbath laws? | He said it misunderstands the Bible's ancient audience and purpose; God gave what was meaningful for relationship with him in that cultural setting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=9897" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:57</a> | If Jesus intervened for the sins of other beings such as extraterrestrials, would he have multiple bodies? | He said the issue belongs to speculative theology and depends on whether such beings fell, were deceived, and share solidarity with humanity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=10371" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:51</a> | Did biological death exist anywhere in creation before the fall? | Yes. He distinguished earlier physical death from the later proliferation of death caused by the fall of humanity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=10621" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:57:01</a> | Did God believe there were actually other gods, or were they angels and spirits? | He said ancient Israel used "gods" broadly for spirit beings under Yahweh, not as equal deities in polytheism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=10941" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:21</a> | Are spirits considered gods, such as a spirit of lust or gluttony? | He said spirit beings can be called little-g gods, but he did not think there are necessarily specific spirits assigned to lust or gluttony. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11032" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:52</a> | Are you a theistic evolutionist? | No. He said he is neither a theistic evolutionist nor a creationist. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11064" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:04:24</a> | Why do you use Monero or Bitcoin? | He said some donors in the Middle East needed privacy because supporting the channel through normal banking was illegal for them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11152" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:52</a> | Is your view henotheism? | No. He distinguished henotheism from monarchial monotheism, where one supreme God rules over subordinate spirit beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11302" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:08:22</a> | What is your opinion on the Elephantine papyri? | He said the Elephantine community was an Israelite settlement near Aswan that fell into aberrant worship practices. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11375" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:09:35</a> | What are your thoughts on Greg Boyd's work on Old Testament violence? | He said he remembered reading something by Boyd that seemed good and evenhanded, but he could not recall the details clearly. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11442" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:10:42</a> | Do we have historical evidence for the schools of the prophets that Heiser and Walton discussed? | He said no external evidence apart from Kings, though the concept makes sense in a society where professions often followed family lines. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=11808" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:48</a> | Is the twice-yearly sun event at Abu Simbel intentional? | He said maybe, but the evidence is uncertain because the two temples have different axes and the event is not recorded at the site. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=12098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:21:38</a> | Did the Israelites at Elephantine worship a goddess they believed was Yahweh's spouse? | He said they did worship a goddess alongside Yahweh, but he thought it was more Canaanite influence than clear syncretism into Yahweh's spouse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=12308" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:25:08</a> | Why did God take pleasure in killing animals in the Old Testament, and is killing animals sinful? | He said God did not take pleasure in animal sacrifice, but sacrifice and animal slaughter were treated as necessary and not sinful. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=12541" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:01</a> | Can Genesis, especially the creation account, be considered mythological because of problems with the regional flood theory? | He said the question was poorly framed because "myth" is too broad and flood-model problems do not determine whether Genesis is mythological. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=12759" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:39</a> | Did the authors of Genesis incorporate myth to show Yahweh behind the myth story? | He said Genesis is more than simple myth or oral tradition; it is a foundational ritual and religious text shaped for Israel's theology. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=13019" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:59</a> | Was the Adam and Eve story symbolic, including a literal tree? | He said it can be both historical and symbolic, with real Adam, Eve, and disobedience carrying theological meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=13294" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:41:34</a> | After Jesus and the New Covenant, was chosenness transferred to the multiethnic body of Christ as spiritual Israel? | He said chosenness remains with Abraham's seed, while election and access to the salvific message move to the church, avoiding supersessionism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=13538" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:45:38</a> | What would we expect to see if Genesis was not historical at all and was meant as mythological? | He said this is extremely difficult to quantify because ancient myth, history, ritual, and truth claims often overlap. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/jPxGwjQc-B4?t=14038" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:53:58</a> | Did ancient pagan kingdoms preserve records of the Tower of Babel and Nimrod's kingdoms? | He said the Tower of Babel would predate writing, and Nimrod likely functions as a literary amalgamation of several Mesopotamian kings. |
